name: Yayoi Kusama
artStatement: My art originates from hallucinations only I can see. I translate the obsessive visions and the infinite nets of dots that have flooded my mind since childhood into paintings, sculptures, and environments. Through repetition and accumulation I seek the self-obliteration of the individual into the infinite — a love for the universe that erases the boundary between body and cosmos.
education: Kyoto Municipal School of Arts and Crafts — Nihonga painting (1948–1949)
Art Students League of New York (1958)
awards: U.S. Department of State Medal of Arts (2012)

interviews.title: Studio Visit: Inside the Infinity Mirror Rooms

press.title: How Yayoi Kusama Conquered the Art World
Inside the Mind of the Polka-Dot Queen

books.title: Infinity Net: The Autobiography of Yayoi Kusama
Yayoi Kusama: Every Day I Pray for Love

publicCollections: The Museum of Modern Art, New York
National Museum of Modern Art, Tokyo

series.name: Infinity Nets
series.description: Vast monochrome canvases built from repeated, hand-painted loops — the foundational gesture of accumulation and self-obliteration.

series.name: Infinity Mirror Rooms
series.description: Immersive mirrored environments filled with lights and forms that dissolve the viewer into endlessly reflected space.

works.title: Infinity Mirrored Room — The Souls of Millions of Light Years Away

series.name: Pumpkins
series.description: The artist's beloved motif rendered as paintings and monumental polka-dot sculptures — humble, humorous, and deeply personal.

name: El Anatsui
artStatement: I work with media that have a history of human touch — discarded bottle caps, printing plates, milk-tin lids. By stitching thousands of fragments together with copper wire I make works that are deliberately non-fixed: they can be draped, folded, and re-formed at every installation. Form should remain free. My materials carry the memory of trade, consumption, and the meeting of cultures across the Atlantic.
education: BA Sculpture, University of Science and Technology, Kumasi, Ghana (1968)
Postgraduate Diploma in Art Education, Kumasi (1969)
awards: Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement, Venice Biennale (2015)

press.title: El Anatsui Turns Bottle Caps Into Gold
The Sculptor Who Drapes Buildings in Metal

publicCollections: The Metropolitan Museum of Art, New York
Smithsonian National Museum of African Art, Washington D.C.

series.name: Bottle-Top Hangings
series.description: Monumental shimmering 'cloths' made from flattened liquor-bottle caps stitched with copper wire — metal that flows like fabric.

series.name: Wooden Wall Sculptures
series.description: Chainsaw-carved and scorched wooden panels exploring Adinkra symbols, language, and rupture.

name: Julie Mehretu
artStatement: My paintings are palimpsests — layered maps of architecture, migration, protest, and weather. I build dense fields of marks, gestures, and erased photographic source images so that history accumulates and dissolves at once. I am interested in the social agency of abstraction: how a non-figurative surface can hold the pressure of contemporary events without illustrating them.
education: MFA, Rhode Island School of Design (1997)
awards: U.S. Department of State Medal of Arts (2015)
publicCollections: The Museum of Modern Art, New York
Whitney Museum of American Art, New York
San Francisco Museum of Modern Art

series.name: Stadia
series.description: Explosive arena-like compositions of flags, banners, and architectural fragments evoking spectacle and global crowds.

series.name: Grey Area
series.description: Quieter, smoke-like canvases where marks are sanded and erased — abstraction approaching atmosphere and loss.

series.name: Recent Paintings
series.description: Large works built over blurred photographic images of protest and conflict, layered with airbrushed colour and gesture.
